active_answer: in case of emergency, or for ties that may need adjustments along the way
active_question: Despite being less solid than other locking methods, when does Rope 365 say quick-release locks may be useful?
reason: The existing Q&A clearly retains the source’s two useful cases for a less-solid quick-release lock.
support_evidence: A quick-release is a way to create a temporary lock that is very fast to tie and untie. They may not be as solid as other locking methods, but they are great in case of emergency, or for ties that may need adjustments along the way. We just need to be extra careful in case it unties accidentally with movement.

active_answer: a half hitch on the loop created by the first half hitch
active_question: What locks the exit of a slipped half hitch into place?
reason: The existing Q&A accurately identifies the extra half hitch that locks the slipped half hitch’s exit.
support_evidence: A slipped half hitch is made by not pulling the rope completely through and tightening the hitch. You’ll need to start from a change of direction or some friction on the hitch’s origin as the entry point will not be locked. Then we make a half hitch on the loop created by the first half hitch to lock the exit into place.

active_answer: capacity to open and close the hands
active_question: When tying the arms behind the back with a quick-release cuff, what capacity does Rope 365 say to monitor?
edited_answer: the ability to open and close the hands
edited_question: When a quick-release cuff binds the arms behind the back, what hand function does Rope365 say to monitor?
paraphrase_rationale: The revised Q&A names hand function directly and replaces the awkward ‘what capacity’ construction while preserving the motor check.
reason: The revised Q&A names hand function directly and replaces the awkward ‘what capacity’ construction while preserving the motor check.
support_evidence: For a more restrictive experience, you can tie the arms in the back using the quick release cuff. As we tie on the arms, we have to be mindful of nerve impingement, monitoring the capacity to open and close the hands and make use of the quick release to mitigate those risks.

active_question: Where does Rope 365’s checklist say cuffs should be located on the body?
reason: The existing Q&A clearly states the checklist’s joint-and-nerve avoidance requirement for cuff placement.
support_evidence: - All cuffs are located in a safe place on the body, away from joints and nerves.
